intent: Define the SecurityRecipes model-provider routing gate: a deterministic policy layer that decides which model provider and model class may receive secure context for a governed agentic workflow, with explicit proof for data retention, residency, training exclusion, guardrails, telemetry, run receipts, and human approval before context crosses a frontier, local, or unsanctioned model boundary.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Product Security, GRC, privacy, procurement security, model risk, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Secure model-provider routing and context boundary governance for agentic AI
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes makes model choice easy to approve: a team can ask for the best model for an agentic workflow while the routing gate proves whether the provider, model, data class, autonomy level, and evidence state are acceptable.
positioning.why_now: Enterprises are moving from single-model pilots to multi-provider agent fleets that combine MCP tools, private code, ticket data, browser activity, remote agents, and frontier-model reasoning. A secure context layer needs a provider-neutral routing decision before sensitive context reaches any model boundary.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Routes account for goal hijack, tool misuse, privilege abuse, context poisoning, insecure inter-agent communication, cascading failures, excessive autonomy, and rogue-agent containment before a model receives context or authority.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP MCP Top 10
standards_alignment.coverage: Model routing is treated as a context and tool boundary where model misbinding, context spoofing, prompt-state manipulation, insecure memory references, and covert channel risks must be reviewed.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10
standards_alignment.coverage: The routing gate links model choice to skill governance, publisher verification, permission review, isolation, update drift, and runtime audit before behavior packages execute under agent authority.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization
standards_alignment.coverage: Model routes require MCP resource binding, least-privilege scope selection, audience validation, HTTPS, PKCE, token validation, and no token passthrough before tool-backed context crosses the provider boundary.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Routing evidence requires confused-deputy prevention, scope minimization, SSRF controls, session safety, audit trails, and explicit treatment of local and remote MCP server risk.

standards_alignment.name: OpenAI: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
standards_alignment.coverage: Routes require source-sink thinking, constrained impact, user confirmation for sensitive transmissions, and layered controls because social-engineering-style prompt injection cannot be solved by input filtering alone.

standards_alignment.name: OpenAI Agents SDK Guardrails
standards_alignment.coverage: Routing evidence requires input, output, and tool guardrails at the right workflow boundaries, including blocking mode where tool execution or token spend should not begin before a tripwire clears.

standards_alignment.name: OpenAI Agents SDK Tracing
standards_alignment.coverage: Provider decisions bind model calls, tool calls, handoffs, guardrails, and custom events to trace evidence without storing raw prompts, outputs, or tool payloads by default.

standards_alignment.name: Anthropic Claude Code Security
standards_alignment.coverage: Routes preserve read-only defaults, explicit approvals, trusted MCP server configuration, isolated execution, command review, and secure credential handling for coding-agent workflows.

standards_alignment.name: CISA AI Data Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Provider routing requires data provenance, integrity, access control, monitoring, third-party handling, incident evidence, and protections for sensitive operational AI data.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
standards_alignment.coverage: The gate maps provider routing into governed, mapped, measured, and managed AI risk, including critical-infrastructure trust expectations emerging in 2026.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
standards_alignment.coverage: Routes require generative-AI governance, data provenance, third-party dependency review, monitoring, cybersecurity, incident response, and operational risk management.

provider_profiles.title: Approved frontier model provider under enterprise contract
Local developer model for public or synthetic context only
Unsanctioned provider, personal account, or unmanaged agent host

model_route_profiles.title: Public secure-context reasoning through approved frontier provider
Tenant remediation route through approved frontier provider
Private runtime for restricted tenant context
Guardrail classifier for browser and untrusted-content routing
Shadow AI or personal-account model route

workflow_route_overrides.notes: Sensitive-data runs should prefer private runtime and may use approved frontier routes only after data-class, redaction, and approval evidence are present.
Dependency remediation can use public vulnerability intelligence freely, but repository-specific context requires tenant-sensitive route evidence.
SAST triage frequently contains source slices, traces, and exploitability reasoning; route as tenant sensitive by default.

commercialization_path.open_layer: Public provider-routing profile, deterministic generator, runtime evaluator, docs, and MCP tools for teams standardizing model selection evidence.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Customer-specific provider profiles, approved model registry, data-class maps, regional policies, model eval baselines, and route exceptions.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Hosted model-routing PDP, provider contract inventory, policy-as-code adapters, route telemetry, procurement exports, and SIEM/SOAR alerts for unsanctioned model use.
commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, AI coding platform, cloud platform, or security vendor gets the missing enterprise approval layer between secure context, MCP tools, and multi-provider model execution.

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes can prove that agentic workflows send the right context to the right model provider under the right contract before autonomy expands.
default_questions_answered: Which model providers are approved for public, tenant-sensitive, regulated, or browser-derived context?
Which data classes are prohibited for each provider or model route?
Which routes require zero data retention, training exclusion, DPA, residency, guardrails, telemetry, receipt, and human approval evidence?
Which workflows can use frontier hosted models versus private runtimes?
Which runtime signals force hold, deny, or kill decisions before a model call starts?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach the routing gate to AI platform model registries, secure-context retrieval, MCP gateway policy, context egress, procurement review, and enterprise trust-center exports.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open model-route policy, then sell hosted route decisions, provider inventory, contract-state checks, tenant-specific policy packs, and acquisition diligence reports.

residual_risks.risk: The open profile cannot verify a customer's live provider contract or tenant data-processing terms.
residual_risks.treatment: Bind provider profiles to procurement systems, legal contract metadata, provider trust-center evidence, and tenant-specific exception workflows.

residual_risks.risk: A model route can be safe at admission but become unsafe after a provider, model, region, retention, or tool policy change.
residual_risks.treatment: Recompute route hashes, require approval for route drift, and connect this gate to telemetry, run receipts, and provider inventory change events.

residual_risks.risk: Provider-neutral model IDs are intentionally abstract in the open project.
residual_risks.treatment: Fork the profile and map route IDs to approved OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, private-cloud, or local model SKUs inside the enterprise layer.
